name: BEND FLY SHOP
address: 50 SE SCOTT STREET STE # 1, BEND, OR 97702

fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and efficiency could be achieved by developing alternative access routes to complement the existing single access point. Introducing secondary points of entry or adjusting the perimeter fencing layout may enable more options for responder movement to the main building and adjacent areas. Maintaining clear markings for the vehicle parking area can assist in rapid identification and use of staging locations. Regularly trimming and managing the landscaped area will facilitate unobstructed navigation and visibility for emergency personnel. Additionally, ensuring that clear signage is in place and implementing measures that optimize side access may improve overall effectiveness during emergency operations.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure could help reduce potential fire exposure to the building. Managing the vegetated perimeter by thinning and reducing the density of trees can minimize fuel continuity and support the creation of buffer zones around the property. Ensuring that vegetation is strategically spaced and maintained may contribute to the reduction of fire spread opportunities. Regular maintenance of the vegetated perimeter, including pruning and clearing of dead or dry vegetation, could further enhance property preparedness. Incorporating these practices can support improved firefighting access and aid suppression operations, contributing to long-term wildfire resilience.
